block_01: It seems that the stock market close of Quarter 4 & the close of Quarter 1, could not tell stories any more different.
block_02: At the end of 2018, the markets took a tumble, with the Dow Jones reaching lows unseen for 10 years. In addition the S&P 500 was down 19.8% from its peak, barely missing the 20% mark necessary to classify it as a “true” bear market.
block_03: However such harsh falls, coupled with 2019’s strong open; largely due to the U.S. & China trade war, which now seems to be finding a more positive ground. As well as the FED (Federal Reserve System) presenting a dovish sentiment early in the year, building further confidence in the U.S. economy; has led to a strong rebound, with a clear distinction between the then, & now.
block_04: Once again, the knock on effect of news, whether it be political, local or otherwise, is a major factor for the movements we see in the global markets. This further strengthens the justification of applying fundamental analysis to trading decisions; in order to mitigate risk & allow for more calculated decisions.
